svg.title: Key Roles in the Futures Industry
svg.desc: Study infographic explaining customers, APs, IBs, FCMs, CTAs, CPOs, principals, exchanges, the CFTC, the NFA, and their relationships.
svg.text: Key Roles in the Futures Industry
Who does what in the U.S. futures and derivatives ecosystem

text.tspan: an FCM, IB, CTA, or CPO.
- Main brokerage firm in the market.
- Similar to fund manager for a pool.
or controlling person of a firm.
- Senior person tied to firm control.
- Organized marketplace where futures and options on
- Provides trading rules, contract specifications, and
- Central place where buyers and sellers meet.

svg.text: RELATIONSHIP DIAGRAM - THE ECOSYSTEM AT A GLANCE
Route D: Investors -> CPO -> Pool -> FCM -> Exchange
IB introduces and solicits; FCM carries accounts and holds funds.
CTA gives advice; CPO pools investor money and operates a pool.
NFA is the industry self-regulator; CFTC is the federal regulator.
AP is usually client-facing; Principal is supervisory or control-oriented.
